claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes with project-scoped evidence requirements.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines evidence schema with scope field and four valid scopes: project, external, user, unknown.
evidence.why: This is the core evidence schema that mandates scope declaration.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES Set validates only four scopes are allowed: project, external, user, unknown.
evidence.why: This enforces scope enumeration at the schema level.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false and result_schema: yolo.demand.evidence_result.v1.
evidence.why: This establishes the three-agent evidence protocol with scope-aware result schema.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function classifies evidence by scope field and source, with explicit mapping of project_code, project_test, project_docs, project_config, project_log, project_artifact to 'project' scope.
evidence.why: This is the core scope classification logic that maps source types to scopes.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues functions block evidence records without declared scope and require project-scoped evidence with concrete locators.
evidence.why: This enforces scope declaration requirements for PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues blocks claims when evidence is non-project (external/user/unknown only) for existing-project facts, and requires repo-relative path for project-scope evidence.
evidence.why: This is the core validation that prevents external-only evidence from satisfying project facts.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for complete evidence results, blocking PRD readiness for scope or locator violations.
evidence.why: This integrates scope validation into the readiness contract.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates agent prompts with explicit scope rules, requiring project evidence with repo-relative paths and marking external research as scope=external.
evidence.why: This communicates scope requirements to evidence agents in their protocol prompt.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates each evidence record declares scope as project, external, user, or unknown and blocks during result normalization.
evidence.why: This enforces scope validation at dispatch time when processing agent results.

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' verifies external evidence blocks PRD readiness with PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker.
evidence.why: This confirms the readiness contract rejects external-only evidence for project facts.

evidence.summary: Test 'project evidence requires a concrete project locator' verifies project-scope evidence without path fails with PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: This confirms project-scope evidence requires repo-relative path or file locator.

evidence.summary: Test 'external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence' verifies mislabeled external URLs with scope=project fail with PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED.
evidence.why: This confirms URLs cannot satisfy project evidence even with project scope label.

evidence.summary: Test 'evidence records must declare valid scope explicitly' verifies evidence without scope field fails with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: This confirms scope declaration is mandatory for all evidence records.

evidence.summary: Test 'agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization' verifies dispatch blocks agents that return evidence without scope field.
evidence.why: This confirms dispatch runtime enforces scope validation during agent result processing.

evidence.summary: Test 'cross-check PRD readiness requires every evidence agent result' verifies all three agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) must complete with project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: This confirms the three-agent protocol with scope validation is enforced by readiness.

risks: If evidence scope validation is bypassed or disabled, agents could cite external sources as project facts.
If boundary enforcement fails, agents could modify project files during evidence gathering.

result.notes: All four components (schema, prompt, readiness contract, tests) explicitly distinguish evidence scopes and enforce project-scoped evidence requirements. The schema defines four scopes, the prompt communicates scope rules to agents, the readiness contract validates scope declarations and project locators, and tests verify external-only evidence cannot satisfy project facts.
